title: M6: CLI - all clap subcommands with --json output
M6.1: CLI structure - clap subcommands with --json flag
description: Implement CLI with clap: main command with global --json, --config, --port flags. Subcommands: start, stop, status, health (server management). Group: provider (list, add, remove, enable, disable, test). Group: combo (list, create, delete, update). Group: key (list, create, revoke, activate, deactivate). Group: pool (list, add, test, remove). Group: alias, quota, usage, config. All support --json for machine-readable output.
close_reason: Implemented CLI structure with clap subcommands

title: M6.2: CLI server commands - start, stop, status, health
description: Implement server management CLI: openproxy start [--port 20128] [--config path]. openproxy stop (signal server shutdown). openproxy status [--json] returns {status, port, uptime, connections, combos}. openproxy health returns {ok, uptime, connections, combos}. Start contacts HTTP API to register, or manages PID file.
close_reason: Implemented server commands: start, stop, status, health

title: M6.3: CLI provider/key/pool commands
description: Implement provider CRUD CLI: provider list [--json], provider add --name glm --key sk-xxx --models ..., provider remove <id>, provider enable/disable <id>, provider test <id>. Implement key management: key list, key create [--name], key revoke/activate/deactivate <id>. Implement pool management: pool list, pool add --name --url --type, pool test/remove <id>.

title: M6.4: CLI route command - direct invoke without HTTP
description: Implement openproxy route command: openproxy route --model cc/claude-opus-4-7 --prompt 'hello' [--stream]. Direct invoke of proxy logic without HTTP. Load db, resolve model, call executor, stream response to stdout. For testing and scripting. Support combo: openproxy route --combo default --prompt '...' [--stream].

title: M6.5: Shell completion - bash/zsh/fish scripts
description: Implement shell completion: generate bash comp script, zsh comp function, fish comp. Clap shell_complete attribute. Install instructions in CLI help. Completion for all subcommands: provider, combo, key, pool, alias, quota, usage, config, route. Flags: --json, --config, --port.

title: M2: Provider Executors - Claude, Gemini, Kiro, special providers
close_reason: done

title: M2.1: ClaudeExecutor - Anthropic format translation (OpenAI <-> Claude)
description: Implement ClaudeExecutor for anthropic/claude provider. Format translation: OpenAI /v1/chat/completions -> Anthropic /v1/messages. Map messages, system, tools, thinking. Response: Anthropic SSE -> OpenAI SSE (content blocks -> delta). Handle cache_control (anthropic-beta format). OAuth tokens from providerConnection.accessToken.
close_reason: done

title: M2.1a: Unit tests for ClaudeExecutor
description: Write unit tests for ClaudeExecutor: OpenAI -> Anthropic message transformation, system messages, tools, thinking. Anthropic SSE -> OpenAI SSE content blocks transformation. cache_control injection. OAuth token handling. Test streaming with thinking blocks.
close_reason: done

title: M2.2: GeminiExecutor - Google AI format with function calling
description: Implement GeminiExecutor for gemini providers. OpenAI tools -> Google function declarations. Map OpenAI message format to generateContent contents/parts. Handle system instructions. Support vision (image URLs). Streaming via server-sent events with gemini-verbose output. Include apiKey in URL param or Authorization header.
close_reason: done

title: M2.2a: Unit tests for GeminiExecutor
description: Write unit tests for GeminiExecutor: OpenAI tools -> Google function declarations, message to contents/parts transformation, system instruction handling. Vision image URLs. apiKey in URL vs Authorization header. Streaming response parsing.
close_reason: done

title: M2.3: KiroExecutor - AWS Signature v4 signing + EventStream decoding
description: Implement KiroExecutor for kiro provider. AWS Signature v4 signing: canonical request, string to sign, signature calculation. Handle EventStream binary format -> SSE conversion. Custom model lock mechanism. Per-request signing with proper nonce. Credentials from providerConnection.accessToken (AWS credentials).
close_reason: done

title: M2.3a: Unit tests for KiroExecutor
description: Write unit tests for KiroExecutor: AWS Sig v4 signing, canonical request, string to sign, signature calculation. EventStream binary -> SSE decoding. Per-request nonce. Token refresh. Test with mock AWS credentials.
close_reason: done

title: M2.4: VertexExecutor - Google Service Account JWT auth
description: Implement VertexExecutor for vertex providers. Service Account JWT auth: create JWT with RS256, sign with SA private key, exchange for access token. Dual URL patterns: standard vertex AI endpoint and partner endpoints. Project/location parsing from baseUrl. Token refresh before expiry.
close_reason: done

title: M2.4a: Unit tests for VertexExecutor
description: Write unit tests for VertexExecutor: Service Account JWT creation, RS256 signing, token exchange. Dual URL patterns (SA JSON vs raw key). Project/location parsing. Token refresh before expiry. Test with mock service account credentials.
close_reason: done

title: M2.5: CodexExecutor - OpenAI Responses API format
description: Implement CodexExecutor for codex provider. Responses API format (different from chat completions). Handle image URLs (prefetch). Extract reasoning.effort. Requires local TCP proxy on port 1455 for OAuth callback interception. Use Authorization header with Bearer token from providerConnection.accessToken.
close_reason: done

title: M2.6: CursorExecutor - Connect Protocol gRPC over HTTP
description: Implement CursorExecutor for cursor provider. Connect Protocol RPC over HTTP/2. Frame format with gzip support. XML serialization for tool_result. Build service/method URLs. Handle streaming responses. Cursor uses local config.db import (no OAuth). Map cursor models to openai models.
close_reason: done

title: M2.7: OllamaExecutor - Ollama /api/chat format
description: Implement OllamaExecutor for ollama-local and ollama-cloud providers. /api/chat format with messages array. Handle image extraction from base64 data URIs. Map model names (llama3, mixtral, etc). Stream parameter for SSE. URL construction from providerNodes or baseUrl.
close_reason: done

title: M2.8: GrokWebExecutor + PerplexityWebExecutor - Cookie auth
description: Implement GrokWebExecutor (grok-web with sso= cookie) and PerplexityWebExecutor (perplexity-web with Pro/Max cookie). Cookie-based auth parsing from providerConnection.cookie. Grok uses app-chat/conversations/new endpoint. Perplexity uses SSE /rest/sse/perplexity_ask with session cache (FNV-1a hash of conversation history). backend_uuid for conversation continuity.
close_reason: done

title: M2.9: Response format translation - streaming SSE transformation
description: Implement streaming format translation for each executor pair. Transform provider SSE events to OpenAI SSE format in real-time. Handle chunk-by-chunk processing with bytes::Bytes zero-copy. Manage streaming state: base state (id, model, role) + extra state per format. Support: Claude thinking blocks, Gemini functionCall, Ollama native thinking, Kiro event types.
close_reason: done

title: M2.9a: Unit tests for streaming format translation
description: Write unit tests for streaming format translation: chunk-by-chunk processing, bytes::Bytes zero-copy, streaming state management (base state + extra state). Test: Claude thinking blocks, Gemini functionCall, Ollama native thinking, Kiro event types. Verify no string allocation.
close_reason: done

title: M3: Account + Combo System - fallback, locking, round-robin
close_reason: Implemented account fallback, locking, round-robin, combo strategies

title: M3.1: Account fallback - per-account locking and rate-limit tracking
description: Implement per-account fallback: markAccountUnavailable(account, model, cooldownMs) locks account+model. isModelLockActive checks lock expiry. filterAvailableAccounts excludes rate-limited accounts. resetAccountState clears cooldown on success. Track errorCode, consecutiveUseCount, lastError, lastErrorAt on providerConnection.

title: M3.1a: Unit tests for account fallback
description: Write unit tests for account fallback: markAccountUnavailable locks account+model, isModelLockActive checks expiry, filterAvailableAccounts excludes locked, resetAccountState clears cooldown. Test consecutiveUseCount increment, errorCode tracking, lastError persistence.

title: M3.2: Model lock mechanism - modelLock_<model> timestamp tracking
description: Implement model lock mechanism: modelLock_<model>: timestamp for per-model locks, modelLock___all: timestamp for all-model locks. isModelLockActive checks model-specific then account-level. Locks auto-expire based on timestamp comparison with current time. Return should_skip for locked models in getProviderCredentials.

title: M3.3: Round-robin strategy - comboRotationState tracking
description: Implement round-robin for combos: comboRotationState Map tracks current index per combo. getRotatedModels() returns next model in sequence. Round-robin also applies to providerStrategies (fill-first vs round-robin vs sticky). stickyRoundRobinLimit setting. Reset rotation on success or after limit.
close_reason: Implemented round-robin combo tracking with strategies

title: M3.4: Proxy pool selection - per-account proxy + connection proxy
description: Implement proxy pool selection: check connectionProxyEnabled + connectionProxyUrl on providerConnection. Support proxyPoolId reference to proxyPools. connectionNoProxy pattern matching (comma-separated, wildcard). Global outbound proxy from HTTP_PROXY/HTTPS_PROXY env vars. Vercel relay support via vercelRelayUrl. Use hyper or reqwest Proxy extension.

title: M3.5: RTK compression - token saver with content detection
description: Implement RTK (token) compression: detect content type from first 1KB of tool_result. Compress git-diff, git-status, grep, find, ls, tree. Dedup log entries. Smart truncate long content. Safety: if compression grows output, keep original. Settings.rtkEnabled toggle. Handle multiple message shapes (OpenAI Responses API, OpenAI tool messages, Claude blocks arrays).
close_reason: not-needed

title: M3.5a: Unit tests for RTK compression
description: Write unit tests for RTK compression: detect git-diff, git-status, grep, find, ls, tree content. Compress and verify size reduction. Safety: if compression grows output, keep original. Test multiple message shapes (OpenAI Responses API, OpenAI tool messages, Claude blocks arrays). rtkEnabled toggle.

title: M3.6: Provider strategy settings - fill-first/round-robin/sticky
description: Implement provider strategies: settings.providerStrategies map (fill-first/round-robin/sticky per provider). settings.stickyRoundRobinLimit (1-10). Combo strategies: settings.comboStrategy + settings.comboStrategies per combo. UI in profile page for routing strategy configuration. Persist to db.json.

title: M8: Usage + Pricing - usage.json tracking and cost calculation
close_reason: done

title: M8.1: usage.json tracking - request/response logging
close_reason: done

title: M8.2: Pricing calculation - per-model cost from settings.pricing
close_reason: done

title: M8.3: Usage API - /api/usage/* endpoints
close_reason: done

title: M1: Core HTTP Server with /v1/chat/completions
close_reason: done

title: M1.1: Project setup - Cargo.toml + directory structure
description: Set up Rust project structure with Cargo.toml containing all required dependencies: axum, tokio, hyper, reqwest, clap, simd-json, serde, jsonwebtoken, bytes, arc-swap, tracing, etc. Create src/ subdirectories: cli/, server/api/, server/dashboard/, server/auth/, core/proxy/, core/combo/, core/executor/, core/translator/, core/rtk/, core/auth/, core/model/, db/, oauth/, types/
close_reason: Completed: Cargo manifest resolved, Rust module scaffold created, binary/lib boot path compiles, verified by cargo metadata/check/test.

title: M1.1a: Unit tests for Cargo.toml dependencies
description: Write unit tests verifying all Cargo.toml dependencies compile correctly: axum routing, tokio async, hyper client, simd-json parsing, jsonwebtoken JWT, arc-swap lock-free, bytes zero-copy, tracing subscriber. Test feature flags. Verify no conflicting dependencies.
close_reason: Completed: dependency smoke tests cover routing, async runtime, hyper client, reqwest, JSON/JWT, arc-swap, bytes, tracing, URL/query, chrono, uuid, and aws-sigv4; cargo test and cargo check --all-targets pass.

title: M1.10: GET /v1/models endpoint
description: Implement GET /v1/models returning list of available models. Build from active providerConnections (filter isActive=true) + customModels. Format as OpenAI models response: {object: 'list', data: [{id, object: 'model', ...}]}. Model IDs should be provider/model format.
close_reason: Implemented /v1/models endpoint with active-provider, combo, custom-llm, dedupe behavior

title: M1.10a: Unit tests for /v1/models endpoint
description: Write unit tests for GET /v1/models: returns list from active providerConnections + customModels, model IDs are provider/model format, correct OpenAI format {object: 'list', data: [{id, object: 'model'}]}. Test filtering by isActive. Test with no providers.
close_reason: Added integration-style tests for OpenAI response shape, dedupe, and empty-list behavior

title: M1.11: POST /v1/chat/completions endpoint
description: Implement POST /v1/chat/completions entry point. Parse model from request body. Check if combo -> handleComboChat(). Parse model string -> getProviderCredentials (filter by provider, exclude model-locked). Call handleChatCore -> executor.execute() -> SSE streaming response. Handle errors with fallback to next account.
close_reason: Implemented POST /v1/chat/completions with auth, model resolution, combo/account fallback, and streaming passthrough

title: M1.11a: Integration test for /v1/chat/completions
description: Write integration test for POST /v1/chat/completions: full flow from request to streaming response. Mock provider server. Test model parsing, combo detection, executor selection, streaming response, error handling with fallback. Test with real db.json. Verify SSE format to client.
close_reason: Added wiremock integration tests for streaming, account fallback, combo fallback, cooldowns, and model-aware selection

title: M1.12: Caveman context compression - lite/full/ultra levels
description: Implement Caveman request preprocessing: settings.cavemanEnabled + cavemanLevel (lite/full/ultra). Inject Caveman prompts into OpenAI-, Claude-, and Gemini-shaped request bodies before provider dispatch while preserving existing guidance blocks. Apply heuristically only when estimated context pressure crosses a model-aware threshold. This establishes the Caveman stage that coexists with RTK; the separate RTK token-compression pass remains tracked under br-2g8.5.
close_reason: Implemented Caveman request preprocessing with multi-shape injection, level normalization, heuristic auto-detect, and regression coverage.

title: M1.2: DB layer - db.json + usage.json read/write with arc-swap
description: Implement the DB layer with arc-swap for lock-free reads. Load ~/.openproxy/db.json and usage.json on startup. Implement atomic file writes after modifications. Use arc-swap::ArcSwap for hot path reads (provider lookup, auth check) and RwLock for writes. Must maintain compatibility with original db.json schema.
close_reason: Completed: typed db.json/usage.json persistence with ArcSwap snapshots, tolerant top-level JSON repair, atomic writes, usage normalization/migration, and .9router-first compatibility verified by cargo test/check.

title: M1.2a: Unit tests for DB layer
description: Write unit tests for DB layer: load db.json + usage.json, arc-swap read performance, RwLock write contention, atomic file write (no partial state on crash), db.json schema validation, migration from old format. Test round-trip serde_json serialize/deserialize for all types.
close_reason: Completed: DB tests cover load/normalize, tolerant legacy repair, usage migration, serialized concurrent updates, snapshot reads, update_usage persistence, atomic write cleanup, and serde round-trips via cargo test.

title: M1.3: Type definitions - all data structures matching db.json schema
description: Define all Rust structs matching the db.json schema: ProviderConnection (oauth/apikey/cookie variants), ProviderNode, ProxyPool, Combo, ApiKey, Settings, Pricing. Use serde for JSON serialization. Include model alias resolution, custom models, mitm alias. All types must round-trip through serde_json without data loss.
close_reason: Completed: Rust schema types now cover provider connections, nodes, proxy pools, combos, API keys, settings, pricing, custom models, mitm aliases, and usage summary shapes with unknown-field preservation.

title: M1.3a: Unit tests for type definitions
description: Write unit tests for type definitions: ProviderConnection serialize/deserialize for oauth/apikey/cookie variants, model alias resolution, combo serialization, settings with all fields, pricing calculation. Verify serde attributes, optional fields, default values. Test edge cases: empty strings, null values, nested objects.
close_reason: Completed: serde tests cover app/usage round-trips, null-tolerant legacy sections, auth variants, proxy pools, custom models, mitm aliases, pricing, defaults, and unknown-field preservation.

title: M1.4: Model resolution - parse provider/model strings, resolve aliases
description: Implement model string parsing: 'cc/claude-opus-4-7' -> (provider='kiro', model='claude-opus-4-7'). Handle model aliases from settings.modelAliases. Check if model is a combo reference. Parse combo names (prefix 'combo:' or just name). Return resolved model info for proxy routing.
close_reason: Completed: model resolution now supports provider aliases, compatible-node prefixes, top-level aliases, plain combo names, combo: prefixes, and inferred provider fallback based on the existing JS behavior.

title: M1.4a: Unit tests for model resolution
description: Write unit tests for model resolution: parse 'cc/claude-sonnet-4.5' -> (provider='kiro', model='claude-sonnet-4.5'), resolve aliases from modelAliases, detect combo references, handle invalid formats, default provider for short model names. Test combo name parsing (combo:name or just name). Edge cases: empty model string, missing provider.
close_reason: Completed: model-resolution tests cover alias parsing, combo detection, combo: prefix handling, compatible nodes, inferred fallback, unknown aliases, empty input, and missing-model edge cases.

title: M1.5: Default Executor - passthrough for OpenAI-compatible providers
description: Implement DefaultExecutor for 40+ API key providers (openai, deepseek, groq, xai, mistral, etc). Interface: buildUrl(model, stream), buildHeaders(credentials, stream), transformRequest(model, body), execute(). Most providers are passthrough with header-only modification (Authorization: Bearer). Support stream parameter for SSE.
close_reason: Expanded the current DefaultExecutor passthrough matrix for M1 chat providers, including anthropic/gemini/cline/kilocode/cloudflare-ai/opencode-go URL and header handling, compatible-node overrides, and execute-path coverage.

title: M1.5a: Unit tests for Default Executor
description: Write unit tests for DefaultExecutor: buildUrl for openai/deepseek/groq, buildHeaders with Bearer token, passthrough body transformation, stream parameter handling. Test provider-specific URL patterns. Mock HTTP responses. Verify no body modification for passthrough providers.
close_reason: Added execute-path, passthrough, stream-flag, beta-URL, and negative credential/header coverage for DefaultExecutor

title: M1.6: Per-provider hyper::Client pools with keepalive
description: Implement per-provider client pools with keepalive. Preserve reqwest pools for proxy and complex provider paths, and add a lower-overhead hyper direct path for simple non-proxied /chat/completions traffic. Tune pool_max_idle_per_host and tcp_keepalive, isolate pools by provider/proxy fingerprint, and prove connection reuse across repeated requests.
close_reason: Implemented per-provider pooling with keepalive and a direct hyper hot path for simple chat completions, with reqwest fallback for proxy and complex routes.

title: M1.6a: Unit tests for per-provider client pools
description: Write unit tests for per-provider pools: connection pool isolation, keepalive settings, pool_max_idle_per_host tuning, TLS handshake reuse. Test concurrent requests across providers. Verify no pool contention. Mock hyper client responses.
close_reason: Covered current reqwest client pool behavior: single-init per key, documented tuning, provider/proxy isolation, concurrent provider traffic independence, and HTTP keep-alive reuse.

title: M1.7: API key auth - validate x-api-key header or Authorization Bearer
description: Implement API key validation middleware. Extract key from x-api-key header or Authorization: Bearer. Look up in db.apiKeys. Check isActive flag. Machine ID based token validation for CLI usage (x-9r-cli-token header). Return connection ID or error 401.
close_reason: Added x-9r-cli-token auth path with machine-id validation while preserving bearer and x-api-key precedence

title: M1.7a: Unit tests for API key auth
description: Write unit tests for API key auth: validate x-api-key header, validate Authorization: Bearer, invalid key returns 401, inactive key returns 401, valid key passes through. Test x-9r-cli-token header validation with machineId. Test key lookup performance with many keys.
close_reason: Added unit and integration coverage for CLI-token auth, machine-id mismatch, precedence, and many-key lookup

title: M1.8: SSE streaming - bytes::Bytes zero-copy with backpressure
description: Implement SSE streaming with zero-copy using bytes::Bytes. Provider SSE -> bytes_stream() -> Body::from_stream(). Implement pipeWithDisconnect() for format translation during streaming. Client disconnect -> abort upstream request. Support chunk-by-chunk format transformation for OpenAI SSE -> client SSE.
close_reason: done

title: M1.8a: Unit tests for SSE streaming
description: Write unit tests for SSE streaming: bytes::Bytes zero-copy, chunk-by-chunk transformation, backpressure handling, client disconnect triggers abort. Test pipeWithDisconnect with mock provider stream. Verify no string allocation per chunk. Test format translation during streaming.
close_reason: done

title: M1.9: Combo handler - fallback + round-robin strategies
description: Implement combo handling: check if model is a combo reference, parse combo name. Support fallback strategy (try in order, stop at first success) and round-robin (rotate evenly). Track comboRotationState map for round-robin. Fallback triggers: 401->120s cooldown, 429->exponential backoff, 502/503/504->retry. Call handleSingleModelChat for each model in sequence.
close_reason: Combo execution is implemented and now exercised through the real chat route as well as combo unit tests

title: M1.9a: Unit tests for combo handler
description: Write unit tests for combo handler: fallback strategy stops at first success, round-robin returns next model in sequence, comboRotationState map updates correctly. Test error triggers: 401 cooldown, 429 backoff, 502/503/504 retry. Test getProviderCredentials with exclude set for locked models.
close_reason: Added explicit transient fallback coverage plus direct account-selection tests for excluded and locked credentials

title: M9: Advanced Features - MITM, tunnel, observability
M9.1: MITM proxy - antigravity, copilot, kiro interception
description: Implement MITM proxy: intercept tool calls for antigravity, copilot, kiro. TLS mitm with self-signed cert. DNS bypass for known intercepted hosts. Model mapping via mitmAlias. Decode request/response. Forward to real provider. Serve from /api/cli-tools/antigravity-mitm. Trust cert flow. DNS toggle per tool.

title: M9.2: Tunnel/Tailscale support - cloudflare tunnel, tailscale funnel
description: Implement tunnel support: cloudflare tunnel (tunnelEnabled, tunnelUrl, tunnelProvider=cloudflare). Tailscale Funnel (tailscaleEnabled, tailscaleUrl). Check status via /api/tunnel/status. Enable/disable via /api/tunnel/enable|disable. Tailscale management: check, install, enable, disable. SSE streaming for install/enable operations.

title: M9.3: Observability - request logging with tracing
description: Implement observability: settings.observabilityEnabled toggle. Collect request logs with tracing. Max records (default 10000), batch size (100), flush interval (5000ms), max JSON size (100000). Log client request, provider request, provider response, client response. Timing: TTFT, total latency, token counts. Store in memory ring buffer. Export via /api/usage/request-logs.

title: M9.4: Cloud sync init - /api/init endpoint
description: Implement cloud sync init: GET /api/init for cloud sync setup. Settings.cloudEnabled toggle. Tunnel-based access. May involve cloudflare zero-trust or similar. Store cloud config in settings. Background sync of db.json to cloud storage.

title: M9.5: MITM UI dashboard - per-tool MITM configuration
description: Implement full MITM dashboard UI: MitmServerCard (cert status, trust cert, start/stop server), per-tool MITM cards (antigravity, copilot, kiro). DNS status badge, hosts entries, model mappings (alias -> target). Password modal for sudo actions. TLS mitm with self-signed cert generation.

title: M9.6: Vercel relay deploy UI
description: Implement Vercel relay deploy UI: deploy edge relay function for IP masking. Use /api/proxy-pools/vercel-deploy endpoint. Progress indicator during deploy. Instructions for Vercel API token setup. Status check after deploy.

title: M4: OAuth Flows - PKCE, device_code, import_token
M4.1: PKCE authorization_code flow - claude, codex, gitlab
description: Implement PKCE authorization_code flow for claude, codex, gitlab. Generate code_verifier (32 bytes random) + code_challenge (base64url(sha256(code_verifier))). Redirect to authorization URL. Handle callback with code exchange. Store code_verifier for token exchange. Store accessToken, refreshToken, expiresAt, idToken in providerConnection.

title: M4.1a: Unit tests for PKCE flow
description: Write unit tests for PKCE flow: code_verifier generation (32 bytes), code_challenge base64url(sha256). Authorization URL construction. Callback handling with code exchange. Token storage in providerConnection. Error handling: invalid code, expired code.

title: M4.2: Device code flow - github, kiro, kimi-coding, kilocode
description: Implement device_code flow for github, kiro, kimi-coding, kilocode, codebuddy. POST to device authorization endpoint. Poll device code endpoint with interval. Handle SlowDown error (increase interval). Post-exchange processing: github needs extra POST to /copilot_internal/v1/token. Kiro requires AWS SSO OIDC (client registration first).

title: M4.2a: Unit tests for device code flow
description: Write unit tests for device code flow: device authorization request, polling with interval, SlowDown error handling (increase interval). Post-exchange: github copilot token fetch. Kiro special handling (client registration first). Test token storage.

title: M4.6: GitHub Copilot special handling - device_code + copilot_internal token
description: Implement GitHub special flow: device_code -> then POST to /copilot_internal/v1/token to get Copilot token. Store token in providerConnection.accessToken. Headers: Authorization: Bearer <token>. Use for codex, copilot providers. Handle session management.

title: M4.3: import_token flow - cursor SQLite config.db import
description: Implement cursor import_token flow: read from local SQLite config.db file. Parse access_token, refresh_token, expires_at. Calculate checksum for validation. No OAuth server - just local file import. Store tokens in providerConnection. Note: no token refresh (cursor uses long-lived tokens).

title: M4.3a: Unit tests for cursor import
description: Write unit tests for cursor SQLite import: read config.db, parse access_token, refresh_token, expires_at. Calculate checksum. Store tokens in providerConnection. No refresh (long-lived tokens). Handle missing file, corrupted db.

title: M4.4: Token refresh system - auto-refresh before expiry with retry
description: Implement token refresh system: TOKEN_EXPIRY_BUFFER_MS = 5min. Per-provider refresh implementations in each executor. RefreshWithRetry with exponential backoff. Trigger in handleChatCore before executor.execute(). Store new tokens in providerConnection and persist to db.json. Handle 401 (re-auth required), 429 (rate limited, wait).

title: M4.5: Kiro AWS SSO OIDC - register client, device auth, token
description: Implement Kiro special flow: AWS SSO OIDC. Step 1: client registration (client_id, client_secret). Step 2: device authorization (device_code, user_code). Step 3: token poll (device_code, device_auth_pending). Dual refresh path: access_token refresh vs re-do full flow. Store client_id, client_secret in providerConnection.providerSpecificData.

title: M4.7: GitLab OAuth - authorization_code_pkce + PAT support + dynamic baseUrl
description: Implement GitLab OAuth: authorization_code + PKCE for oauth, also support PAT (personal access token) as fallback. Dynamic baseUrl for self-hosted GitLab instances. Store accessToken, refreshToken. Use for gitlab provider in providerNodes.

title: M5: Full Provider Coverage - all 40+ providers
close_reason: Implemented full provider coverage architecture for 40+ providers: Created unified ProviderExecutor trait and UnifiedExecutor struct in src/core/executor/provider.rs with PROVIDER_REGISTRY containing all API key, OAuth, and free providers. Built comprehensive provider configuration including base URLs, headers, auth types, and format handling. All 204 tests pass, clippy clean (warnings are pre-existing).

title: M5.1: API key provider executors - 40+ passthrough providers
description: Implement DefaultExecutor for all 40+ API key providers: openai, anthropic, deepseek, groq, xai, mistral, perplexity, together, fireworks, cerebras, cohere, nebius, siliconflow, hyperbolic, deepgram, assemblyai, nanobanana, chutes, ollama-local, vertex-partner, gitlab, codebuddy, opencode-go, azure, cloudflare-ai, blackbox, glm, glm-cn, kimi, minimax, minimax-cn, alicode, alicode-intl, volcengine-ark, byteplus. Most are passthrough with Authorization: Bearer header. Some need special baseUrls from providerNodes.

title: M5.2: Web cookie providers - grok-web, perplexity-web cookie auth
description: Implement cookie-based auth for grok-web (sso= cookie) and perplexity-web (Pro/Max cookie). Parse cookie from providerConnection.cookie. grok-web: app-chat/conversations/new endpoint. perplexity-web: SSE /rest/sse/perplexity_ask with session cache (FNV-1a hash), backend_uuid for conversation continuity.
close_reason: Implemented web cookie providers for grok-web and perplexity-web

title: M5.3: Media providers - TTS, STT, embedding, image, search
description: Implement media providers: elevenlabs, cartesia, playht, deepgram (STT); google-tts, edge-tts (TTS); openai, cohere, voyage-ai (embedding); dalle, stable-diffusion (image); tavily, brave-search, serper, exa (search). Map /v1/audio/*, /v1/embeddings, /v1/images/generations, /v1/search to provider-specific formats. Passthrough or light transformation.

title: M5.4: Free tier providers - openrouter, nvidia NIM, ollama cloud, vertex, gemini
description: Implement free tier handling: openrouter (27+ free models, passthrough), nvidia NIM (passthrough), ollama cloud (same as ollama-local), vertex ( credits, SA auth), gemini (15 RPM, 1M tokens/day 2.5-flash, apiKey in URL). No credit card required. Handle rate limits gracefully.

title: M5.5: ProviderNodes (custom endpoints) - prefix routing, apiType
description: Implement ProviderNodes support: custom API endpoints with prefix routing (e.g. 'my/' maps to custom provider). Fields: id, type, name, prefix, apiType (openai/claude/anthropic/vertex/ollama), baseUrl. Used for opencode-go, openrouter-compatible, self-hosted endpoints.
close_reason: Implemented ProviderNodes with prefix routing and apiType support

title: M5.6: Media providers - TTS/STT/embedding/image/search UI
description: Implement media providers dashboard: /dashboard/media-providers/[kind]/[id] for elevenlabs, cartesia, playht, deepgram (STT); google-tts, edge-tts (TTS); dalle, stable-diffusion (image); tavily, brave-search (search). Web media providers: /dashboard/media-providers/web. Media provider combo editor.

title: M7: Dashboard - SolidJS + embedded UI
M7.1: SolidJS UI - component library with routing
description: Set up SolidJS + Vite + UnoCSS for dashboard. Create src/ with App.tsx, index.tsx, index.css. Components: DashboardLayout (sidebar nav), ProviderCard, ComboCard, UsageChart, QuotaDisplay, SettingsForm. Routing via @solidjs/router. Store via zustand. Build to dist/ for embedding. Focus on fast runtime (~7KB) and compiled-away reactivity.

title: M7.2: Dashboard routes - all 12+ pages
description: Implement all dashboard routes: /dashboard (endpoint + API keys), /dashboard/providers (grid + test), /dashboard/providers/[id] (connections + models), /dashboard/combos (drag-reorder), /dashboard/usage (charts), /dashboard/quota (60s refresh), /dashboard/profile (settings), /dashboard/mitm, /dashboard/cli-tools, /dashboard/proxy-pools, /dashboard/basic-chat, /dashboard/console-log. Real-time updates: tunnel polling 2000ms, quota countdown 60s.

title: M7.3: Dashboard API integration - all /api/* endpoints
description: Implement dashboard frontend API calls: /api/providers (CRUD), /api/combos (CRUD), /api/keys (CRUD), /api/proxy-pools (CRUD + test), /api/settings (GET/PATCH), /api/usage/* (stats, chart, history), /api/tunnel/* (status, enable/disable), /api/oauth/* (authorize flows). Handle auth: JWT cookie, API key header.

title: M7.4: Brotli compression + embedding in binary
description: Implement brotli precompression for dashboard assets. Build dist/ with all HTML/CSS/JS. Compress with brotli. Embed via include_dir! in Rust. Serve with correct Content-Encoding: br header. Fallback to uncompressed if client doesn't support brotli. Target ~60-70% size reduction. Dashboard should add ~1.5-3MB to binary.

title: M7.5: Theme switcher + database export/import UI
description: Implement theme switcher (light/dark/system) and database export/import in profile page. Theme persistence in localStorage. Export db.json download, import file upload with validation. Settings profile page: require login toggle, password change, routing strategy, outbound proxy, observability toggle.

title: M7.6: Basic chat interface + console log viewer
description: Implement basic-chat UI: /dashboard/basic-chat - simple chat interface for testing. Console log viewer: /dashboard/console-log - displays request/response logs, errors, debugging info. Real-time log streaming from observability data. Log level filtering.

title: M7.7: Translator interface UI
description: Implement translator UI: /dashboard/translator - translation format testing interface. Select source format (OpenAI, Claude, Gemini, etc.) and target format. Test format conversion with sample data. Debug format translation issues.

title: M7.8: Pricing configuration UI
description: Implement pricing UI: /dashboard/settings/pricing - configure per-model pricing (input/output/cached/reasoning costs). Default prices per provider. Import/export pricing config. Calculate estimated cost per request based on token counts.
